subject: mathematics

core_competencies.justification: The whole chapter builds the real-number system step by step — whole numbers and zero, integers and the need for negative numbers, rational numbers in standard form and on the number line, irrational numbers discovered through the diagonal of a unit square, and the decimal classification that separates terminating, recurring, and non-recurring expansions — which is exactly the understanding of the set of real numbers and its properties that this competency names.

adjunct_competencies.justification: The eight-step proof that √2 is irrational argues from a stated assumption (a coprime p/q form) through definitions to a contradiction, which is the kind of proof from stated assumptions and definitions this competency describes.

Converting a recurring decimal back to p/q form, and the proof of √2's irrationality, both break a single claim into an ordered sequence of smaller justified steps, which is the decomposition this competency names.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to classify and place any number within the real number system by tracing each number set — whole, integer, rational, irrational — back to the property that distinguishes it from the sets before it.
